---
title: "final"
author: "Hambee Makinoda"
date: 2026-03-04
format:
  html:
    toc: true 
execute:
  message: false 
  warning: false 
---

[GitHub Repo](https://github.com/Hambanana0923/ENVS-193DS_winter-2026_final)

# Set Up

```{r}
#| label: reading in packages/data

```

# Problem 1: Research writing

## a. Transparent statistical methods

In part 1, they used a simple linear regression test, because they were testing whether one continuous predictor variable, precipitation, predicts another continuous variable, flooded wetland area.\
\
In part 2, they used a Kruskal–Wallis test, because they were comparing the median flooded wetland area across more than two groups: wet, above normal, below normal, dry, and critical drought. Differences in median can best be measured by nonparametric test, which in this case is Kruskal-Wallis test instead of ANOVA test.
